in_distribution_eval.note: Fresh tasks drawn from the training prior. The model distinguishes the two classes cleanly (positive mean logit > 1.3, negative < -1.3) and reaches 77% accuracy on the synthetic distribution.

zero_shot_breast_cancer.note: Zero-shot in-context classification. PFN has never seen the dataset; it predicts purely from the 48-sample context in each of 30 bootstrap passes (probabilities averaged per test point). LogReg fits the full 426-sample train fold.

reproducibility.note: Numbers above are byte-identical across reruns on the same machine + same versions. With torch + numpy seeded by hyperparams.seed (42), each `priorstudio run` of this study produces these exact values. Cross-machine FP noise may shift the final digits but not the headline (zero-shot accuracy ~80-85%, AUC ~0.85-0.90, gap to LogReg ~15 points). See README § Reproducibility.

notes: Headline: zero-shot 83.2% accuracy / AUC 0.87 on a real medical dataset, training only on synthetic random-hyperplane tasks.

Still 15 percentage points behind LogReg (which fits on the same labelled data). Closing that gap is what richer priors (BNN-generated tasks, SCM mixtures — like TabPFN's actual prior set) buy you. Out of scope for this study.

Two critical pipeline bugs fixed during this study and one determinism fix added afterward: (1) _default_step + checkpoint walker now find nn.Modules nested as `mod._linear` / `mod.proj`, so embedder + head weights actually train (and persist); (2) train_pfn passes `seed + step*batch_size` to sample_batch instead of `seed + step`, so each task is seen exactly once across training (recency overfitting fixed); (3) train_pfn and the local adapter both call torch.manual_seed early, so same-machine reruns are byte-identical.
